color_profiles.name: Classic Black Obsidian
color_profiles.cause: Iron and titanium oxides (magnetite) distributed throughout glass matrix.

color_profiles.name: Rainbow Obsidian
colors: black with rainbow iridescence bands — green
color_profiles.cause: Thin layers of oriented magnetite nanoparticles creating thin-film interference.

color_profiles.name: Snowflake Obsidian
color_profiles.cause: Devitrification spherulites of cristobalite (silica) and feldspar crystallizing post-emplacement.

color_profiles.name: Mahogany Obsidian
color_profiles.cause: Iron oxide-rich zones (hematite/goethite) banding through black glass.

color_profiles.name: Apache Tears
color_profiles.cause: Small rounded nodules of obsidian that weather from perlite matrix; translucent in thin section.

visual_notes: Obsidian is pure volcanic glass — the most visually simple and the most dramatically effective black mineral material. Its jet-black vitreous surface reflects light cleanly. Rainbow varieties produce full spectral color from thin-film magnetite layers. Apache tears appear opaque but transmit brownish light when held up.

optical_effects.iridescence: Rainbow obsidian — full spectral iridescence from nanolaminate magnetite interference layers
optical_effects.play_of_color: Rainbow obsidian — full spectral iridescence from nanolaminate magnetite interference layers
optical_effects.chatoyancy: Sheen obsidian — silver or gold sheen from parallel nanoparticle layers
